context_lines: 1 Peter 5:1-2 should be read within the flow of its chapter and the larger argument of 1 Peter.
common_interpretation_lines: Elder, shepherd, overseer — again the same men, the same task, described three ways. Not a hierarchy. A job, seen from three angles: age and maturity (elder), responsibility for watching over souls (overseer), and the actual daily work of tending people (shepherd).

And notice something else in every one of these passages: the word is always plural. Elders. Overseers. Never a solitary bishop over a region. Every church Paul planted got a plurality of men recognized for this work — not one man elevated above the rest.
